channel.title: Differential-Forms on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Differential-Forms on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: Differential Forms: The Natural Language of Integration on Manifolds
item.description: In vector calculus on $\mathbb{R}^3$ , we have three derivative operations: gradient ($ abla f$ ), curl ($ abla imes F$ ), and divergence ($ abla \cdot F$ ). Each operates on a different type of object (scalar fields, vector fields). Two identities — $ abla imes ( abla f) = 0$ and $ abla \cdot ( abla imes F) = 0$ — sit there looking like happy coincidences. The three integral theorems (the fundamental theorem for line integrals, the classical Stokes’ theorem, the divergence theorem) appear unrelated and unmotivated except by their statements.
